questions.html: Which code keeps running while total is less than 10?
questions.notice: while total < 10: — don't forget the colon, and the loop stops as soon as total reaches 10

questions.html: Which code keeps running while lives is greater than 0?
questions.notice: while lives > 0: — the loop continues as long as lives is at least 1

questions.html: Which code keeps running while password is not equal to 'open'?
questions.notice: while password != 'open': — != means 'not equal to', so the loop keeps going until the correct password is entered

questions.html: Which code keeps running while count is less than or equal to 5?
questions.notice: while count <= 5: — <= means less than OR equal to, so the loop includes when count is exactly 5

questions.html: Which code keeps running while score is equal to 0?
questions.notice: while score == 0: — use == to compare, not = which is for assignment

questions.html: Which code keeps running while health is greater than or equal to 50?
questions.notice: while health >= 50: — >= means greater than OR equal to, so the loop includes when health is exactly 50

questions.html: Which code keeps running while attempts is less than 3?
questions.notice: while attempts < 3: — the loop runs when attempts is 0, 1 or 2, then stops when it reaches 3

questions.html: Which code keeps running while balance is not equal to 0?
questions.notice: while balance != 0: — the loop keeps going until balance reaches exactly 0

questions.html: Which of these is a valid first line of a while loop?
questions.notice: while speed > 30: — Python uses 'while' not 'whilst', no brackets needed, and always needs a colon

questions.html: Which code keeps running while temperature is less than 100?
questions.notice: while temperature < 100: — the loop stops as soon as temperature reaches 100
